title: Relationships
text: Relationships A relationship is an association between one entity and another. It is modeled as a development object belonging to the many-side entity in a one-to-many relationship. The Relationship object specifies the primary or candidate keys to be used in maintaining the association, as well as the delete constraints to be used when deleting occurrences in the related entities. Uniface supports one-to-many relationships and uses these as the basis for modeling one-to-one and many-to-many relationships. One-to-one relationships can be defined by a one-to-many relationship in which the foreign key in the many entity is a candidate key. Many-to-many relationships must be implemented by creating an intermediate entity. For example, an employee can be assigned to multiple projects and a project can involve many employees. In the following illustration, the Employment entity handles the many-to-many relationship between an employee and projects. Figure 1. Modeling Many-to-Many Associations Related Links Referential Integrity Referential integrity is a standard feature of relational database design that prevents users or applications from entering inconsistent data. Most relational databases have referential integrity rules that you can apply when you create a relationship between tables. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
